description_long: This structure is a traditional trullo, characterized by its thick, roughly textured stone walls and a large, steeply pitched conical roof made of light-colored tiles. The exterior walls are constructed from irregularly shaped stones, giving the building a rustic and historic appearance. There are small, arched doorways and windows set into the stone, with one main entrance accessed by a short set of stone steps. The roof is smooth and uniform, contrasting with the roughness of the walls, and lacks any decorative pinnacle at its apex. The trullo is compact and squat, with a broad base and a sharply tapering roof, typical of vernacular Apulian architecture. The overall color palette is dominated by pale stone and white, with subtle variations in shading due to the natural materials.
description: A compact, stone-walled trullo with a steep conical roof and small arched entrances, featuring a rustic, historic appearance and pale coloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched doorway, stone steps, and a window set into the thick stone wall.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting a secondary arched opening and the continuation of the stone steps, with the conical roof prominent above.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a single small arched window and the uninterrupted stone wall beneath the conical roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a small arched window and the stone steps visible on the far side, emphasizing the building's symmetry.
